title: Track A Unattended Review Verdict
description: Written by the fresh-context review step to phases/{p}/review-verdict.json (2026-07-13-unattended-review-gate.md, Key entities). The review step judges only; it writes NOTHING except this file. The gate is the review step's OWN verify: scripts/check-review-verdict.ps1 (sha256-pinned in the plan's verify command) checks (1) verdict=='PASS', (2) reviewed_head==current HEAD, (3) the bookkeeping-excluded dirty set equals exactly {review-verdict.json}. Ported from interactive/core/procedures/schemas/review-result.schema.json (track B). Delta vs track B: reviewed_spec_sha256 REMOVED (track B pinned the spec by hash; track A pins the whole committed repo state by HEAD sha instead); reviewed_head ADDED (necessary clause #3 — binds the verdict to the impl feat commit the engine creates AFTER the impl session ends, so it cannot be pre-forged); optional timestamp/iteration DROPPED (the engine owns retry accounting, not the verdict).

verdict.description: FAIL when one or more MAJOR findings exist. PASS when zero MAJOR findings (MINOR findings may still be listed). The checker gates on verdict=='PASS'.

reviewed_head.description: Output of `git rev-parse HEAD` read at review time (40-hex lowercase). The checker compares it against the current HEAD; a mismatch fails the gate. The impl step's feat commit sha is created by the engine AFTER the impl session ends, so an impl session cannot pre-forge this value.

findings.description: Every violation found, MINOR and MAJOR. Empty array on a clean PASS.

bucket.description: Checklist bucket: B1 ALV+UI (checklist S1-S2), B2 Logic Hygiene + business-logic correctness (S3, S8, and the semantic items S13-S15), B3 Structure+Naming (S4-S7), B4 Platform+Config (S9-S12). The new semantic items (JOIN cardinality / spec conformance / boundary correctness) tag as B2.

severity.description: MINOR = non-blocking. MAJOR = blocks the gate (>= 1 MAJOR forces verdict FAIL).

object.description: Name of the object (or src/ file) the violation was found in.

finding.description: What is violated, citing the checklist item / convention and the offending code location or excerpt.
